city.name: Mumbai
quickFacts: Famous for Vada Pav and street food

alerts.title: Strict Plastic Ban in Maharashtra
alerts.description: Single-use plastics including bags, containers, and packaging are strictly prohibited. Fines up to ₹25,000 for violations. Use cloth bags, paper, or biodegradable alternatives.

alerts.title: Noise Pollution Regulations
alerts.description: Noise levels must not exceed 55dB during day and 45dB at night. Restrictions on loudspeakers, music, and construction. Violators face fines and legal action.

alerts.title: Traffic Violation Penalties
alerts.description: High penalties for traffic violations: No helmet ₹1,000, wrong parking ₹2,000, speeding ₹500-1,000. License suspension possible for repeat offenders.

alerts.title: Alcohol Sale Restrictions
alerts.description: No alcohol sales allowed between 11 PM to 6 AM. Dry day restrictions on election days and major festivals. Possession of liquor in public is illegal.

alerts.title: Meat Ban During Religious Festivals
alerts.description: Meat shops remain closed during certain festivals (Ganesh Chaturthi, Ram Navami, Mahavir Jayanti). Some areas enforce complete meat sales ban on specific days. Vegetarian options recommended.

alerts.title: Construction Activity Time Restrictions
alerts.description: Construction and demolition work prohibited between 9 PM to 6 AM. Weekend restrictions apply. Permits required from Municipal Corporation. Heavy penalties for violations.

alerts.title: Ganesh Chaturthi Festival Etiquette
alerts.description: Respect Ganesh idols during the 10-day festival (Aug-Sept). Modaks (sweet dumplings) are traditional. Remove shoes in homes displaying idols. Participate in Ganapati processions peacefully and respectfully.

alerts.title: Local Food Etiquette in Maharashtra
alerts.description: Respect the sanctity of meals. Eat with right hand in traditional settings. Vada Pav, Pav Bhaji, and Misal Pav are iconic street foods. Try local Maharashtrian cuisine for authentic experience.

alerts.title: Temple Visit Customs
alerts.description: Remove shoes before entering temples. Maintain silence and reverence. Offer flowers and coconuts as per tradition. Accept prasad (blessed food) with right hand. Photography may be restricted in sanctum.

alerts.title: Marathi Language and Respect
alerts.description: Learning basic Marathi phrases is appreciated. The language is official in Maharashtra. Street signs and menus often display Marathi first. Respect local linguistic pride and cultural identity.

alerts.title: Diwali and Festival Celebrations
alerts.description: Diwali is celebrated with lights, fireworks, and oil lamps. Oil baths are traditional on Diwali morning. Limit firecrackers to designated hours. Share sweets with neighbors and friends as a custom.

alerts.title: Holi Festival Traditions
alerts.description: Holi celebrates spring with colors, music, and bonfire. Use natural colors (herbal gulal) instead of synthetic dyes. Share festive sweets like gujhiya. Enjoy the festival peacefully without causing harm.

alerts.title: Local Train Etiquette in Mumbai
alerts.description: The lifeline of Mumbai - avoid peak hours (8-11 AM, 5-8 PM) if possible. Reserved seats for women, elderly, and disabled. Don't block doors. Keep bags close. Follow queue discipline at stations.

alerts.title: Taxi and Auto Rickshaw Tips
alerts.description: Always use metered taxis or pre-booked services. Insist on meter usage. Auto-rickshaws charge fixed rates in Mumbai. Negotiate price beforehand outside metro areas. Keep small change handy.

alerts.title: Monsoon Safety Precautions
alerts.description: Heavy rainfall June-September causes flooding and landslides. Avoid waterlogged areas. Don't venture into swollen rivers or open drains. Stock emergency supplies. Check weather before traveling. Drainage overflows are common.

alerts.title: Beach Safety in Mumbai
alerts.description: Swim only in designated areas with lifeguards present. Avoid beaches after sunset. Strong currents can be dangerous. Don't touch marine life or coral. Use sunscreen and stay hydrated. Check water quality advisories.

alerts.title: Local Market Bargaining Customs
alerts.description: Bargaining is accepted in street markets and small shops. Start at 40-50% of asking price. Be respectful and polite during negotiation. Fixed prices apply in malls and organized retail. Building relationships helps get better rates.
